methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
The ePlane Company is a Series B transportation-sector venture that has raised $14M+ and is targeting $40–50M in new capital. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies: Einride (Series C, $1.8B), Harbinger (Series C, $500M), and REGENT Craft (Series A, $270M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $14M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $27M. Peer-median analysis references the three comparables, which span $270M to $1.8B with a median of $500M, directly applicable given sector and stage alignment. The peer-median method produces a $500M implied valuation.

The two methods produce a wide range. Low-end synthesis (incorporating stage-progression conservatism) supports $23M; median across both methods indicates $263M; high-end (peer-median ceiling) reaches $575M. This dispersion reflects fundamental uncertainty about whether ePlane's stage and capital efficiency should anchor to its funding history ($27M) or to peer valuations at later funding rounds. Resolution requires clarity on unit economics, technology differentiation versus peer set, and capital deployment velocity post-raise. The peer-median method carries more weight if comparable companies reflect true market pricing; stage-progression is most reliable if funding multiples outpace peer dilution effects.
